title: Reflexive Verbs — se & si
description: Learn the two types of Czech reflexive verbs: those using 'se' and those using 'si'. Master conjugation patterns and when to use each.

exercises.prompt: Which reflexive pronoun do you use with 'mýt' (to wash oneself)?

data.question_cz: Které zvratné zájmeno se používá se slovesem 'mýt'?
data.question_en: Which reflexive pronoun is used with the verb 'mýt'?
data.explanation: 'Mýt se' means to wash oneself. 'Se' is the accusative reflexive pronoun used when the action is performed on oneself directly.

exercises.prompt: Which reflexive pronoun do you use with 'kupovat' (to buy for oneself)?

data.question_cz: Které zvratné zájmeno se používá se slovesem 'kupovat'?
data.question_en: Which reflexive pronoun is used with the verb 'kupovat'?
data.explanation: 'Kupovat si' means to buy for oneself. 'Si' is the dative reflexive pronoun used when the action benefits oneself.
For 'já' (I), 'mýt se' conjugates to 'myju se' or 'myji se'. The reflexive 'se' comes after the verb in the 1st person singular.
For 'ty' (you singular informal), 'učit se' conjugates to 'učíš se'. The reflexive particle is placed before the verb in this sentence structure.
For 'on' (he), 'dívat se' conjugates to 'dívá se'. 'Se' is the reflexive pronoun meaning the action reflects back on the subject.

exercises.prompt: Translate into Czech: 'I am learning Czech.' (use učit se)

data.grammar_note: Use 'učit se' + language in adverb form. 'Česky' is the adverb meaning 'in Czech'.

exercises.prompt: Translate into Czech: 'I am buying myself a book.' (use kupovat si)

data.source: I am buying myself a book.
data.grammar_note: Use 'kupovat si' — 'si' is dative reflexive meaning 'for myself'. 'Knihu' is accusative of 'kniha' (book).

data.explanation: For 'my' (we), 'kupovat si' conjugates to 'kupujeme si'. 'Si' can go either before or after the verb.

exercises.prompt: Arrange words to form the sentence: 'I wash myself every morning.'

data.explanation: Czech word order: Já (I) se myju (wash myself) každé ráno (every morning). The reflexive 'se' typically occupies the second position.

exercises.prompt: Listen and write what you hear

data.note: The reflexive pronoun 'se' is pronounced together with the verb

exercises.prompt: Choose the correct form: '___ si kávu.' (He is making himself coffee.)

data.question_cz: Vyberte správný tvar: '___ si kávu.' (On si vaří kávu.)
data.question_en: Choose the correct form: '___ si kávu.' (He is making himself coffee.)
data.explanation: 'On vaří si kávu' — 'vaří' is the 3rd person singular of 'vařit'. The reflexive 'si' means 'for himself'.
